div1.entryFree:
Blast, subst., 1) cold and violent gust of wind: unruly --s wait on the tender spring, Lucr. 869. Lucr. 869 Wint. IV, 4, 111. Wint. IV, 4, 111 R3 I, 3, 259. Mcb. I, 7, 22. Lr. III, 1, 8. IV, 1, 9. Oth. II, 1, 6. symbol of destruction: airs from heaven or --s from hell, Hml. I, 4, 41. --s and fogs upon thee! Lr. I, 4, 321. virtue preserved from fell destruction's b. Per. V, 3, 89.
2) the blowing of a wind instrument: when the b. of war blows in our ears, H5 III, 1, 5. let the general trumpet blow his b. H6B V, 2, 43. Cor. I, 4, 12. for one b. of thy minikin mouth, Lr. III, 6, 45.
